channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Madison Booker and top-seeded Texas outlast SEC rival Tennessee 67-59 to reach Elite Eight
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Texas coach Vic Schaefer got the tense battle he expected between his defensive-minded Longhorns and the highest-scoring team in the country in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Scherzer allows 2 HRs, leaves Blue Jays debut after 3 innings because of right lat soreness
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Three-time Cy Young Award winner Max Scherzer left his debut start with the Blue Jays on Saturday because of soreness in his right lat muscle. “I just didn’t feel like I could really let the ball go,” Scherzer said, adding that he felt the soreness during his pregame warmup.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Min Woo Lee pulls away from Scottie Scheffler and everyone else to lead Houston Open by 4
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Min Woo Lee chipped in for birdie to take the lead and then left Scottie Scheffler and everyone else in his wake Saturday with a 7-under 63, giving him a four-shot lead in the Houston Open as the Australian closes in on his first PGA Tour victory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge homers 3 times as Yanks go deep on Cortes' first 3 pitches and hit 9 HRs vs. Brewers
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Boom! Boom! Boom! Three home runs on the first three pitches. Aaron Judge followed Paul Goldschmidt and Cody Bellinger to combine for unprecedented fireworks Saturday, starting one of the most memorable days of Judge's already indelible career.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Aryna Sabalenka beats Jessica Pegula in Miami Open final for 19th tour title
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Aryna Sabalenka entered the Miami Open final against Jessica Pegula with 18 career titles on her elite resume. The Miami Open crown had proved elusive until Saturday. The No.
item.dc:creator: MARC BERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MJ Iraldi rallies Nova Southeastern past Cal State Dominguez Hills 74-73 in Division II title game
item.description: MJ Iraldi made two free throws with 17.8 seconds left and finished with 27 points to rally Nova Southeastern to a 74-73 victory over Cal State Dominguez Hills in the championship game of the Division II Tournament at the Ford Center.
item.dc:creator: Evansville, Ind., Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Transgender athletes open up about suing Trump administration over bans from girls sports
item.description: Two New Hampshire transgender athletes opened up about their lawsuit against President Donald Trump's administration over recent trans athlete bans in girls sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Betts, top overall seed UCLA look for March Madness success in Elite Eight meeting with LSU
item.description: With back-to-back games of at least 30 points and 10 or more rebounds, UCLA center Lauren Betts certainly appears unstoppable. But, LSU has thwarted the Bruins before — in the Sweet 16 of last years women’s NCAA Tournament. They'll get a rematch on Sunday, this time in the Elite Eight.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Quakes’ Bouda balances faith with performance during Ramadan
item.description: San Jose Earthquakes forward Ousseni Bouda hasn't had anything to eat or drink from sunrise to sundown during first month of MLS season
item.dc:creator: Harold Gutmann, The Mercury News

item.title: Milos Uzan's late bucket on inbound play sends Houston into Elite Eight with 62-60 win over Purdue
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Houston spent time this week practicing an inbound play that coach Kelvin Sampson thought his team might need against Purdue. Milos Uzan, the third option, ran it to perfection.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jazz get blown out by Denver, 129-93
item.description: DENVER (ABC4 Sports) - One more loss by the Utah Jazz will assure the team of its worst record in franchise history, dating all the way back to 1974 in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah HC loses to defending champs in OT, 2-1
item.description: SUNRISE, Fla. (AP) - Sam Bennett scored his second goal of the game off an assist from Brad Marchand at 4:18 of overtime to give the Florida Panthers a 2-1 win over the Utah Hockey Club at Amerant Bank Arena on Friday. By earning one point, Utah is now nine points behind St.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Trump admin refers Maine's 'noncompliance with Title IX' to DOJ amid fight over trans athletes
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday announced it has referred Maine's "noncompliance with Title IX" to the Department of Justice for enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-Penn State star Abdul Carter makes case for No. 1 pick in NFL Draft
item.description: Former Penn State Nittany Lions star Abdul Carter stated his case for the No. 1 pick on Friday as he spoke to the media at his pro day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DePaul women's basketball coach Doug Bruno steps down after 39 seasons
item.description: CHICAGO — Chicago has produced a lot of basketball icons and one of them in Lincoln Park is stepping down — DePaul's longtime women's basketball coach Doug Bruno.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers to make Trump White House visit next month
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers plan to visit President Donald Trump at the White House to celebrate their World Series victory, the team announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Tony Finau out to win second Houston Open
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - Utah native Tony Finau came into this year after going through two pretty big life events. The first was knee surgery back in December.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Ruff, ABC 4

item.title: Stanford football coach Troy Taylor fired after allegations
item.description: (KRON) -- Stanford football head coach Troy Taylor has been fired, team officials announced Tuesday, following a report that he was investigated twice for allegedly mistreating staffers.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, KRON4

item.title: Utah's playoff hopes take big hit with 5-1 loss
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The playoff hopes of the Utah Hockey Club are growing dimmer after a 5-1 loss to the Detroit Red Wings Monday night at the Delta Center.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Sacramento State to hire Mike Bibby as basketball coach, AP source says
item.description: Former Sacramento Kings star Mike Bibby will be hired as the basketball coach at Sacramento State, a person familiar with the decision told The Associated Press on Monday.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Late boxing legend George Foreman had deep ties to this East Bay city
item.description: (KRON) -- Boxing legend and former heavyweight champion George Foreman died last Friday at the age of 76. Foreman was born in Texas. However, after a troubled upbringing, he then moved to the West Coast after ninth grade when he enrolled in President Lyndon B. Johnson's Job Corps work program.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Stephen Curry listed as questionable for Golden State's Tuesday game in Miami
item.description: Stephen Curry is back with the Golden State Warriors after missing the first game of the team's road trip with a pelvic contusion, and could play when his team takes on the Miami Heat in Jimmy Butler's return game Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: San Diego FC dealt first loss in inaugural season
item.description: San Diego FC lost its first game of the season thus far on Sunday when the club fell 2-1 to Austin FC at Q2 Stadium in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: New York girls basketball coach fired for hair-pulling incident with player apologizes
item.description: In a statement shared with local media, Zullo, 81, said he regretted his behavior and offered apologies to the player, her family, the team, the district, and the community.
item.dc:creator: Griffin Haas, NewsNation
